api_surface_description: REST API for a SaaS project management platform. Endpoints include: GET /api/projects/{project_id}, GET /api/projects/{project_id}/tasks, GET /api/users/{user_id}/profile. Authentication via JWT in Authorization header. No explicit object-level authorization checks documented.

Mobile banking API. Endpoints: POST /api/auth/login (username+password), POST /api/auth/refresh (refresh_token), GET /api/accounts/{account_id}/balance. JWT access tokens expire in 15 minutes. Refresh tokens expire in 30 days. No rate limiting on /api/auth/login documented.

E-commerce platform API. Endpoints: GET /api/products (returns full product objects including cost_price, supplier_id, internal_margin fields), POST /api/orders, GET /api/users/me. Authenticated users can browse products. Product response objects are auto-serialized from ORM models without field filtering.

HR platform API. Endpoints: GET /api/employees, PUT /api/employees/{id}/salary, GET /api/reports/payroll, POST /api/admin/bulk-update. Roles: employee, manager, hr_admin. JWT contains role claim. Manager role has access to GET /api/employees but the authorization check on PUT /api/employees/{id}/salary only validates authentication, not role.

Document processing API. Endpoints: POST /api/documents/fetch-url (accepts external URL, fetches content server-side), POST /api/documents/render, GET /api/documents/{id}. The fetch-url endpoint accepts any URL and returns content. Deployed on AWS EC2. No URL validation or allowlist documented.

Internal microservices API exposed via API gateway. Endpoints: GET /api/v1/health (returns version, environment, db_host, db_name), GET /api/v1/debug/config (requires no auth in staging), GET /api/v1/users. CORS policy set to wildcard (*). Swagger UI available at /api/docs in all environments.

Multi-tenant SaaS API for a legal document platform. Endpoints: GET /api/matters/{matter_id}, GET /api/matters/{matter_id}/documents/{doc_id}, POST /api/matters/{matter_id}/notes. Tenant isolation is enforced at the application layer via a middleware that checks if the authenticated user's tenant_id matches the matter's tenant_id. matter_id values appear to be sequential integers.

Developer API platform. Endpoints: POST /api/keys/generate, GET /api/keys/{key_id}, DELETE /api/keys/{key_id}, POST /api/webhooks/verify. API keys are returned once on creation. Password reset flow uses a 6-digit numeric token sent via email with a 24-hour expiry. No lockout after failed reset attempts.

Fintech payment API. Current version: /api/v3/. Legacy endpoints still active: /api/v1/payments/process (no input validation), /api/v2/transfers (deprecated, no auth refresh requirement). Version routing handled by API gateway. No documentation available for v1 or v2. v1 and v2 are not in the public API docs.

E-commerce flash sale API. Endpoints: POST /api/cart/add, POST /api/checkout/reserve, POST /api/checkout/complete, GET /api/inventory/{product_id}. Flash sale items are limited to 1 per user. The reservation endpoint checks inventory and user purchase history at time of reservation. No rate limiting. Inventory check and reservation are non-atomic operations.
